tasks.title: wifi/iwd: Fix iwd backend to use actual D-Bus API
tasks.body:
## Summary
- The iwd backend was written against a non-existent D-Bus API — methods like `GetDevices`, `GetKnownNetworks`, `ForgetNetwork`, and the `Version` property don't exist in iwd
- Rewrote to use the real iwd D-Bus API: `ObjectManager.GetManagedObjects` for discovery, correct `GetOrderedNetworks` return type `a(on)`, argument-less `Network.Connect` and `KnownNetwork.Forget`, and proper signal strength conversion from centidBm to 0-100%

Fixes #171

## Test plan
- [x] Verified iwd is now detected as available on an Arch Linux system with iwd running
- [x] Verified `BuildNetworkList` returns visible networks with correct SSIDs, signal strength, security type, and known network status
- [x] All existing tests pass (`go test ./...`)
- [x] Test connecting to a network via `ActivateConnection`
- [x] Test forgetting a known network via `ForgetNetwork`
- [x] Test toggling wireless on/off via `SetWireless`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Error: The name is not activatable
issues.body:
Trying on Arch Linux with Omarchy. 

Installed with `eget shazow/wifitui` and with `brew install wifitui`. Same result: Both `wifitui` and `wifitui list` return "Error: The name is not activatable"

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements

tasks.title: Check NetworkManager availability, add backend fallback and tests
tasks.body:
### Motivation

- Ensure the app falls back to the iwd backend when NetworkManager's D-Bus service is not actually available even if a client proxy can be constructed. 
- Make backend selection extensible and testable so behavior can be overridden in unit tests.
- Fixes #171 

### Description

- Replace the hard-coded NetworkManager-first initialization with a `backends` slice of constructors and iterate it in `GetBackend()` to return the first successful backend.  
- Add `backend_linux_test.go` with `TestGetBackend_FallsBackToIWDWhenNetworkManagerUnavailable` to verify fallback to the mock/iwd backend when NetworkManager initialization reports an unavailable D-Bus service.  
- In `wifi/networkmanager/networkmanager.go`, force a lightweight `GetPropertyVersion()` call after creating the NetworkManager proxy to detect when the D-Bus service is not actually available, returning `wifi.ErrNotAvailable` for those errors.  
- Add `isUnavailableDBusError()` to classify DBus errors like `ServiceUnknown`, `NameHasNoOwner`, and `The name is not activatable`, and add `TestIsUnavailableDBusError` in `networkmanager_test.go`.  

### Testing

- Ran the new unit tests `TestGetBackend_FallsBackToIWDWhenNetworkManagerUnavailable` and `TestIsUnavailableDBusError` as part of `go test ./...`, and both tests passed.  
- Existing networkmanager unit tests that check device caching still pass after the change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69cd661402c483258cb87b5dbc95904b)

tasks.title: TUI improvements: Width-aware List/Edit, fit to terminal
tasks.body:
Consolidated PR for merging various TUI improvements.

Focused around making List and Edit views be width-aware and take up the entire width of the terminal. Fixes #160.

### Key changes

- **Shared window state**
  - Added `internal/tui/window.go` with `WindowState` and helper methods for:
    - current terminal dimensions
    - fallback width/height logic
    - content-width clamping

- **List view layout refactor (`internal/tui/list.go`)**
  - `ListModel` now stores:
    - shared `window` pointer
    - `ssidColumnWidth`
    - `desiredColumnWidth`
  - Added dynamic column sizing based on actual SSID + icon width (`refreshColumns`).
  - Centralized available-width calculation and list sizing.
  - Title spacing (`WiFi Network ... Signal`) is now recomputed from current column width.
  - Extracted icon selection into `getIcon(...)` helper.
  - Added `newEditModel(...)` so edit screens inherit current window sizing context.

- **Edit view responsiveness (`internal/tui/edit.go`)**
  - Added window-aware constructor: `NewEditModelWithWindow(...)`.
  - Added internal width logic:
    - `availableContentWidth()`
    - `applyWindowWidth(...)`
  - `tea.WindowSizeMsg` handling now updates input widths from terminal size.
  - Details panel width is no longer fixed; it scales with available content width.

- **Top-level model integration (`internal/tui/tui.go`)**
  - `NewModel(...)` now creates a shared `WindowState` and injects it into the list model.
  - Minor message-case restructuring while preserving status clear behavior.

- **Tests**
  - New:
    - `internal/tui/window_test.go`
    - `internal/tui/list_layout_test.go`
  - Updated:
    - `internal/tui/edit_test.go`
    - `internal/tui/list_test.go`
  - Minor newline/format-only cleanup in a few files.

issues.title: ui: Span border full window width
issues.body:
Right now the window border shrinks to fit the content rather than the full width of the window.

Ideally we want to span the full window, and we can also adjust the columns dynamically to take advantage of the increased width when available.

There's a WIP PR that gets this most of the way: https://github.com/shazow/wifitui/pull/135

Just need to review/edit it more thoroughly, possibly a minor rewrite.

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements

tasks.title: list: (5 APs) -> 📡×5
tasks.body:
### Motivation
- Replace the verbose aggregation label like `(5 APs)` with a compact, emoji-based annotation `📡×5` when a network has more than one access point, and show nothing for a single AP for a cleaner list view.
- Make the symbol configurable via theme so users can customize the appearance in `theme.toml` or their own theme files.

### Description
- Update the list renderer to build the count annotation as `fmt.Sprintf(" %s×%d", CurrentTheme.AccessPointIcon, len(i.AccessPoints))` when `len(i.AccessPoints) > 1` (in `internal/tui/list.go`).
- Add a new `AccessPointIcon string` field to `Theme`, wire it into `NewDefaultTheme()` with a default of `📡`, and include `AccessPointIcon = "📡"` in `theme.toml` (in `internal/tui/theme.go` and `theme.toml`).
- Add unit tests verifying the rendered AP annotation for aggregated networks and absence of annotation for a single AP (`internal/tui/list_test.go`), and a test that `LoadTheme` reads the new `AccessPointIcon` field (`internal/tui/theme_test.go`).

### Testing
- Ran the full test suite with `go test ./...` and all tests completed successfully.
- New unit tests exercise rendering and theme loading and passed as part of the test run.

Fixes #164 

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69c7e1c1cb20832580d02e3c4466a250)

issues.title: ui: Improve list view "(3 APs)" annotation
issues.body: I kinda don't love seeing things like "(3 APs)" in the list view. Some ideas of how to improve them: 1. Emojis? Max out on three emojis for APs, so 2 APs would say `📡📡`, 5 APs would still say `📡📡📡➕` maybe? 1 AP would be nothing (since that's implied) Or maybe `📡···+` for 8? 2. `AP✖️3`? Or `📡✖️3`? Or simply `📡3`? `3📡`? `📡·3`? 3. Other ideas? This annotation should be configurable in the theme.

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements

tasks.title: Adding node retired exporting
tasks.body:
Fixes #377 

Also starts the V2 Migration discussed in https://github.com/openstack-exporter/openstack-exporter/issues/385

## Testing with DevStack

### Regression Testing

Checking for any metric formatting changes - there don't seem to be any.

Before

```bash
stack@ip-172-31-12-57:~/openstack-exporter$ curl "http://localhost:9180/metrics" | grep openstack_ironic_node
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100  7147    0  7147    0     0   3052      0 --:--:--  0:00:02 --:--:--  3052
# HELP openstack_ironic_node node
# TYPE openstack_ironic_node gauge
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="7e051f74-0f8f-4576-a482-cbe8ddd55249",maintenance="false",name="node-2",power_state="power on",provision_state="active",resource_class="baremetal"} 1
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="e3ea486b-5499-48a4-ad4d-3dbfd51169c3",maintenance="false",name="node-1",power_state="power off",provision_state="clean failed",resource_class="baremetal"} 1
```

After

```bash
stack@ip-172-31-12-57:~/openstack-exporter$ curl "http://localhost:9180/metrics" | grep openstack_ironic_node
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100  7215    0  7215    0     0    415      0 --:--:--  0:00:17 --:--:--  1795
# HELP openstack_ironic_node node
# TYPE openstack_ironic_node gauge
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="7e051f74-0f8f-4576-a482-cbe8ddd55249",maintenance="false",name="node-2",power_state="power on",provision_state="active",resource_class="baremetal",retired="false",retired_reason=""} 1
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="e3ea486b-5499-48a4-ad4d-3dbfd51169c3",maintenance="false",name="node-1",power_state="power off",provision_state="clean failed",resource_class="baremetal",retired="false",retired_reason=""} 1
```

## Testing Metrics being Filled

Checking metrics before and after retiring a node.

```bash
stack@ip-172-31-12-57:~/openstack-exporter$ curl "http://localhost:9180/metrics" | grep openstack_ironic_node
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100  7215    0  7215    0     0    415      0 --:--:--  0:00:17 --:--:--  1795
# HELP openstack_ironic_node node
# TYPE openstack_ironic_node gauge
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="7e051f74-0f8f-4576-a482-cbe8ddd55249",maintenance="false",name="node-2",power_state="power on",provision_state="active",resource_class="baremetal",retired="false",retired_reason=""} 1
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="e3ea486b-5499-48a4-ad4d-3dbfd51169c3",maintenance="false",name="node-1",power_state="power off",provision_state="clean failed",resource_class="baremetal",retired="false",retired_reason=""} 1
stack@ip-172-31-12-57:~/openstack-exporter$ OS_CLOUD=devstack-system-admin openstack baremetal node set --retired node-1 --retired-reason "Testing"
stack@ip-172-31-12-57:~/openstack-exporter$ curl "http://localhost:9180/metrics" | grep openstack_ironic_node
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100  7221    0  7221    0     0    419      0 --:--:--  0:00:17 --:--:--  1878
# HELP openstack_ironic_node node
# TYPE openstack_ironic_node gauge
openstack_ironic_node{console_enabled="false",deploy_kernel="",deploy_ramdisk="",id="7e051f74-0f8f-4576-a482-cbe8ddd55249",maintenance="false",name="node-2",power_state="power on",provision_state="active",resource_class="baremetal",retired="false",retired_reason=""} 1
```

tasks.title: Fix dashboard update count to include only valid updates(#63)
tasks.body:
Fixes #63.

I originally reported this dashboard inconsistency after seeing it in a local Docker-based setup. Since the fix is small, I opened this PR with a minimal change.

The runtime version summary now only counts updates marked as valid with `.check`, matching the updates shown on the details page.

Tested with:

- `go test ./...`
- Local Docker verification with existing update data

issues.title: Dashboard update count may include incomplete updates after failed publish attempts
issues.body:
Hi! I’m currently doing a POC for my company with this project in a local Docker-based setup, and I noticed what looks like a small dashboard inconsistency.

## Summary

I found a possible dashboard inconsistency after several failed publish attempts in a local Docker setup.

For one runtime version, the summary table showed `# Updates = 4`, but the details page only showed `1` update.

## What I suspect

It seems failed/incomplete publish attempts may leave update directories behind.

The summary count may include those directories, while the details page only shows valid updates.

## Expected behavior

It would be less confusing if the `# Updates` column only counted valid/published updates, so it stays consistent with the details page.

## Offer

If this behavior should be considered a bug, I’d be happy to open a small fix.

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements

tasks.title: fix: prevent cold-manifest 500s after OTA publish
tasks.body:
## Summary

Based on PR #35 by @isaacrowntree — fixes #19 (cold-manifest 500 errors after OTA publish).

### Changes from #35
- Remove premature cache invalidation in `RequestUploadUrlHandler`
- Pre-warm manifest cache (ios + android) after `MarkUpdateAsChecked`
- Cache Expo channel mapping with 60s TTL
- Move `PreWarmManifestCache` into `MarkUpdateAsChecked` so rollback/republish also benefit

### Additional fixes
- **Fix missing cache invalidation** — `UpdateChannelBranchMappingHandler` now invalidates the channel mapping cache when updating a mapping (was missing, causing stale mappings for up to 60s)
- **Add test for cache invalidation** — verifies the handler actually clears the cache by calling the endpoint via the router
- **Add test for PreWarmManifestCache** — verifies all cache layers (lastUpdate, metadata, manifest) are populated

Closes #19, supersedes #35.

## Test plan

- [x] All existing tests pass (`go test ./...`)
- [x] `TestChannelMappingIsCached` — verifies channel mapping cache hit path
- [x] `TestUpdateChannelBranchMappingInvalidatesChannelMappingCache` — verifies handler invalidates cache (calls endpoint via router, not manual `cache.Delete`)
- [x] `TestPreWarmManifestCache` — verifies prewarm populates all cache layers
- [ ] Manual: publish an update and verify the first `/manifest` request returns 200 without cold-start delay

However right after releasing an update, /manifest starts timing out (takes longer than 10s)

Is this a known issue? Are we doing something wrong? 

Looking at the code seems like the only thing which could cause this is high response times from the eas graphql API?

Using Redis or not using still same issue.

issues.title: feature: NO_COLOR support
issues.body:
Hi,

I’d like to suggest adding support for the NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/).

When the NO_COLOR environment variable is set, the application would disable colored output. This is a widely adopted convention across CLI tools and helps improve accessibility and scripting use cases.

`NO_COLOR=1 wifitui`

It might also be interesting to integrate this behavior with the existing theme system, so users can explicitly opt for a colorless theme if desired.

Thanks!

PS: already running wifitui 0.13.0 :)

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements
concerns: accepted patch has no obvious test change

issues.title: There are some metrics defined `Counter` should be `Gauge`
issues.body:
Within the metrics that represent instantaneous states and are not monotonically increasing, there are some defined as Counters. 

Since these metrics are annotated as TYPE: Counter in the exporter's output, certain scrape implementations automatically append suffixes like `_total`, leading to confusion regarding the metrics' names, and thus should be corrected.

I found:

* `neutron_agent_state`: https://github.com/openstack-exporter/openstack-exporter/blob/a9937703684bb7fc5eadbf805354b1903af5cce9/exporters/neutron.go#L168
* `nova_agent_state`: https://github.com/openstack-exporter/openstack-exporter/blob/a9937703684bb7fc5eadbf805354b1903af5cce9/exporters/nova.go#L151
* `cinder_agent_state`: https://github.com/openstack-exporter/openstack-exporter/blob/a9937703684bb7fc5eadbf805354b1903af5cce9/exporters/cinder.go#L254

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements

tasks.title: fix(postgresql): make schema optional and conditionally required in DefaultPrivileges
tasks.body:
### Description of your changes

Fixes `objectType: schema` in `DefaultPrivileges`, which has never produced valid SQL. Previously, the controller unconditionally appended `IN SCHEMA <name>` to the generated query, but PostgreSQL does not permit `IN SCHEMA` when the object type is `SCHEMAS`. Any resource using `objectType: schema` would immediately and permanently fail with:

> cannot create default privileges: pq: cannot use IN SCHEMA clause when using GRANT/REVOKE ON SCHEMAS

This PR fixes the issue without removing schema from the enum (so the valid use case - granting privileges on future schemas remains supported.

- Makes `spec.forProvider.schema` optional in both the cluster-scoped and namespaced CRDs (previously it was required, making `objectType: schema` impossible to use correctly)
- Adds controller-side validation in Observe enforcing the conditional constraint:
  - `objectType` is `table`, `sequence`, `function`, or `type` -> `schema` is required
  - `objectType` is `schema` -> `schema` must not be set
- Fixes `inSchema` to never emit `IN SCHEMA` when `objectType` is `schema`
- Expands examples to cover all `objectType` variants, with the `schema` variant correctly omitting the `schema` field

Fixes #378 

I have:

- [x] Read and followed Crossplane's [contribution process].
- [x] Run `make reviewable` to ensure this PR is ready for review.

### How has this code been tested

Unit tests cover both new validation paths: `schema` required when `objectType` is not `schema`, and `schema` rejected when `objectType` is `schema`. Full suite passes with `go test ./...`

[contribution process]: https://git.io/fj2m9

<details>
<summary>Original description</summary>
<!--
Thank you for helping to improve Crossplane!

Please read through https://git.io/fj2m9 if this is your first time opening a
Crossplane pull request. Find us in https://slack.crossplane.io/messages/dev if
you need any help contributing.
-->

### Description of your changes

<!--
Briefly describe what this pull request does. Be sure to direct your reviewers'
attention to anything that needs special consideration.

We love pull requests that resolve an open Crossplane issue. If yours does, you
can uncomment the below line to indicate which issue your PR fixes, for example
"Fixes #500":

-->

Removes `schema` from the valid `objectType` enum in the `DefaultPrivileges` CRD spec (both `postgresql.sql.crossplane.io` and `postgresql.sql.m.crossplane.io` variants), as `objectType: schema` has never worked - the provider unconditionally includes an `IN SCHEMA` clause in the generated SQL, but PostgreSQL does not allow `IN SCHEMA` when the object type is `SCHEMAS`, causing any resource using this value to immediately and permanently fail with `cannot create default privileges: pq: cannot use IN SCHEMA clause when using GRANT/REVOKE ON SCHEMAS`. This is technically a breaking change to the CRD, but since `objectType: schema` has never produced valid SQL, no working resources can exist that rely on it (as far as I can tell).

Fixes #378 

I have:

- [x] Read and followed Crossplane's [contribution process].
- [x] Run `make reviewable` to ensure this PR is ready for review.

### How has this code been tested

<!--
Before reviewers can be confident in the correctness of this pull request, it
needs to tested and shown to be correct. Briefly describe the testing that has
already been done or which is planned for this change.
-->

The change removes an enum value - no new code paths are introduced. Existing unit tests pass with `go test ./...`

[contribution process]: https://git.io/fj2m9
</details>

issues.title: DefaultPrivileges: objectType: schema generates invalid SQL
issues.body:
### What happened?
<!--
Please let us know what behaviour you expected and how Crossplane diverged from
that behaviour.
-->

Creating a `DefaultPrivileges` resource with `objectType: schema` always fails. The provider generates an `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES ... IN SCHEMA ... GRANT/REVOKE ON SCHEMAS` statement, but PostgreSQL does not allow the `IN SCHEMA` clause when the object type is `SCHEMAS`.

Expected: the resource reconciles successfully, and the role is granted the privilege on the schema

Actual error from the controller:
`cannot create default privileges: pq: cannot use IN SCHEMA clause when using GRANT/REVOKE ON SCHEMAS`

The root cause is in `createDefaultPrivilegesQuery` and `deleteDefaultPrivilegesQuery` (pkg/controller/cluster/postgresql/default_privileges/reconciler.go, lines 193 and 210): `inSchema()` is called unconditionally regardless of objectType, but PostgreSQL only permits `IN SCHEMA` for table, sequence, function, and type - not for schema itself.

### How can we reproduce it?
<!--
Help us to reproduce your bug as succinctly and precisely as possible. Artifacts
such as example manifests or a script that triggers the issue are highly
appreciated!
-->

Apply the following manifest:

```yaml
apiVersion: postgresql.sql.m.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: DefaultPrivileges
metadata:
  name: example-schema-usage
spec:
  forProvider:
    databaseRef:
      name: my-database
    objectType: schema
    privileges:
      - USAGE
    roleRef:
      name: my-role
    schema: public
    targetRole: my-target-role
  providerConfigRef:
    kind: ClusterProviderConfig
    name: my-provider-config
```

### What environment did it happen in?
Crossplane version: `v2.2.0`
provider-sql version: `v0.14.0`

<!--
Include at least the version or commit of Crossplane you were running. Consider
also including your:

* Cloud provider or hardware configuration
* Kubernetes version (use `kubectl version`)
* Kubernetes distribution (e.g. Tectonic, GKE, OpenShift)
* OS (e.g. from /etc/os-release)
* Kernel (e.g. `uname -a`)
-->

---

### Suggested fix

Remove `schema` from the `objectType` enum in the `DefaultPrivileges` CRD spec.

This is technically a breaking change to the CRD, but since `objectType: schema` has never produced valid SQL, no working resources can exist that rely on it (as far as I can tell).

tasks.title: Add feature to get password from Vault
tasks.body:
To use the feature put the following options in config root: 
use_vault: true
vault_address: http://<IP_ADDRESS:<IP_PORT>
vault_role_id: <ROLE_ID>
vault_secret_id: <SECRET_ID>
vault_secret_path: <deployments/dev/user/passwords_yml>
vault_secret_mount_path: <secret_v2>
credential_name_in_vault_secret: <keystone_admin_password>

Closes https://github.com/openstack-exporter/openstack-exporter/issues/412

issues.title: postgresql: Namespaced Extensions are not properly resolving databaseRef
issues.body:
### What happened?

During testing of a namespaced Extension I was unable to get databaseRef to function as it did under a clustered Extension.  After a little digging it seems the namespaced Extension is not using the namespaced Reference [here](https://github.com/crossplane-contrib/provider-sql/blob/master/apis/namespaced/postgresql/v1alpha1/extension_types.go#L50-L58) when compared to the namespaced Grant [here](https://github.com/crossplane-contrib/provider-sql/blob/master/apis/namespaced/postgresql/v1alpha1/grant_types.go#L132-L140) which I believe to be the culprit as to why the referenced resource is not found.

### What environment did it happen in?

Crossplane version: 2.2.0
SQL Provider version: 0.13.0

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements
concerns: accepted patch has no obvious test change
generated-file churn may dominate the patch

tasks.title: feat(postgresql): support WITH INHERIT FALSE on role membership grants (PostgreSQL 16+)
tasks.body:
Closes #359

## What this does

Adds a `withInherit` boolean field to `spec.forProvider` on the PostgreSQL `Grant` resource, enabling `WITH INHERIT FALSE` on role membership grants — a feature introduced in PostgreSQL 16.

```yaml
apiVersion: postgresql.sql.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Grant
spec:
  forProvider:
    withInherit: false   # emits: GRANT admin TO master_user WITH INHERIT FALSE
    roleRef:
      name: master-user
    memberOfRef:
      name: admin-role
```

When `withInherit` is omitted, behaviour is unchanged. When combined with `withOption: ADMIN`, the emitted SQL is `WITH ADMIN OPTION, INHERIT FALSE`.

The field is only valid on `memberOf` grants — setting it on a privilege grant (`privileges` field) returns a validation error.

## Motivation

The primary use case is RDS PostgreSQL with IAM database authentication. Without this field, granting a master user membership in an `admin` role that holds `rds_iam` creates a transitive chain (`master_user → admin → rds_iam`), which causes RDS to route all connections for that user through PAM/IAM token verification, breaking password authentication. `WITH INHERIT FALSE` grants membership (satisfying `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES FOR ROLE admin`) without inheriting `rds_iam`.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `apis/cluster/postgresql/v1alpha1/grant_types.go` | Add `WithInherit *bool` field to `GrantParameters` |
| `apis/cluster/postgresql/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` | Regenerated |
| `package/crds/postgresql.sql.crossplane.io_grants.yaml` | Regenerated — CRD schema updated |
| `pkg/controller/cluster/postgresql/grant/reconciler.go` | New `membershipWithClauses` helper; `selectGrantQuery` filters `pg_auth_members.inherit_option` when set; `createGrantQueries` uses new helper; validation rejects `withInherit` on privilege grants |
| `pkg/controller/cluster/postgresql/grant/reconciler_test.go` | 7 new test cases covering `WithInherit` nil/true/false for both `Observe` and `Create` |
| `examples/cluster/postgresql/grant-with-inherit-false.yaml` | New example illustrating the RDS IAM pattern |
| `Makefile` | Bump `GOLANGCILINT_VERSION` from `2.1.2` → `2.10.1` to match CI and support Go 1.26 |
| `README.md` | Fix stale Go version (1.18→1.26.1), add mise recommendation, fix copy-paste "Developing locally" section (was provider-helm content), add `Schema` to PostgreSQL resource list, fix example paths |

## Testing

`make reviewable` passes (generate + lint + all 26 test packages).

The `inherit_option` column on `pg_auth_members` was added in PostgreSQL 16. Setting `withInherit` on an older cluster will result in a SQL error, which is the expected and documented behaviour.

issues.title: feat(postgresql): support WITH INHERIT FALSE on role membership grants (PostgreSQL 16+)
issues.body:
## Summary

The PostgreSQL `Grant` managed resource currently supports `withOption` for privilege grants (`WITH GRANT OPTION`), but has no way to express `WITH INHERIT FALSE` on role membership grants — a feature introduced in PostgreSQL 16.

## Background

PostgreSQL 16 added per-grant inheritance control:

```sql
GRANT some_role TO some_user WITH INHERIT FALSE;
```

This grants membership (the grantee can `SET ROLE`) without inheriting the role's privileges automatically. It is distinct from `WITH GRANT OPTION` and cannot be expressed through the existing `withOption` field.

## Use Case

A common Crossplane pattern for RDS PostgreSQL with IAM database authentication:

1. Create an `admin` role with `rds_iam` membership (so migration tools like Flyway/Liquibase/Atlas can connect via IAM tokens).
2. Grant the RDS master user membership in `admin` so it can run `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES FOR ROLE admin`.

On PostgreSQL 15, step 2 creates a transitive chain: `master_user → admin → rds_iam`. This causes the master user's password authentication to break — RDS routes all connections for `rds_iam` members through PAM (IAM token verification), rejecting the regular password.

On PostgreSQL 16, `GRANT admin TO master_user WITH INHERIT FALSE` solves this cleanly: the master user has membership (satisfying the `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES` requirement) but does not inherit `rds_iam` and retains password authentication.

Without this field in the `Grant` CRD, the composition must either omit default privileges management entirely or work around the limitation in ways that push responsibility to the application layer.

## Proposed Change

Add a `withInherit` boolean field to `spec.forProvider` on the PostgreSQL `Grant` resource:

```yaml
apiVersion: postgresql.sql.m.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Grant
spec:
  forProvider:
    role: master_user
    memberOf: admin
    withInherit: false   # emits: GRANT admin TO master_user WITH INHERIT FALSE
```

When `withInherit` is omitted, behavior is unchanged (PostgreSQL defaults to `INHERIT TRUE`).

This field should only be applied when `memberOf` is set (role membership grants). It has no meaning for privilege grants (`privileges` field).

## References

- PostgreSQL 16 release notes: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/16/release-16.html
- `GRANT` syntax: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/16/sql-grant.html
- AWS RDS IAM database authentication: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/UsingWithRDS.IAMDBAuth.html

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements
concerns: generated-file churn may dominate the patch

tasks.title: fix: cache expo auth to prevent ENHANCE_YOUR_CALM rate limit (#58)
tasks.body:
Fixes #58

## Problem
`ValidateExpoAuth()` was making 2 GraphQL calls to api.expo.dev on 
every `/uploadLocalFile` request with no caching. With 527 assets, 
this results in 1054 rapid API calls, triggering Expo's rate limit.
## Fix
Applied the same caching pattern already used in `FetchExpoChannelMapping()` to:
- `FetchSelfExpoUsername()` — cached indefinitely (never changes at runtime)
- `FetchExpoUserAccountInformations()` — cached per token with a short TTL
## Testing
Verified fix resolves the rate-limit error with 527 assets on AWS EKS.

issues.title: ENHANCE_YOUR_CALM rate limit from api.expo.dev on every /uploadLocalFile request due to missing auth caching
issues.body:
**Problem**
When publishing updates with a large number of assets using eoas publish, the server hits Expo's API rate limit (ENHANCE_YOUR_CALM) during file uploads, causing all uploads to fail with 401.
**Error**
Error validating expo auth: Post "https://api.expo.dev/graphql": http2: server sent GOAWAY and closed the connection; LastStreamID=1677, ErrCode=ENHANCE_YOUR_CALM, debug=""

**Root Cause**
ValidateExpoAuth() in internal/services/expo.go makes 2 GraphQL calls to api.expo.dev on every single /uploadLocalFile request with no caching:

FetchExpoUserAccountInformations() — validates the token
FetchSelfExpoUsername() — fetches the server's own username (never changes)
With 527 assets, this results in 1054 API calls in rapid succession, triggering Expo's rate limit.

Note: FetchExpoChannelMapping() already implements caching correctly — the same pattern is missing from the auth validation functions.

**Environment**
Running on AWS EKS (Kubernetes)
527 assets to upload
Works locally (residential IP) — fails on AWS datacenter IP due to stricter rate limiting

**Expected Behavior**
FetchSelfExpoUsername() result should be cached indefinitely (it never changes during runtime). FetchExpoUserAccountInformations() result should be cached per token for a short TTL (e.g. 5 minutes), similar to how FetchExpoChannelMapping() is already cached.

**Suggested Fix**
Apply the same caching pattern already used in FetchExpoChannelMapping() to FetchSelfExpoUsername() and FetchExpoUserAccountInformations().

strengths: issue contains substantive reproduction or requirements
concerns: accepted patch has no obvious test change

issues.title: Support ownerRef for postgres database
issues.body:
### What problem are you facing?
Currently we expect the role name to be set in the `forProvider.owner` for a `Database.postgresql.sql` resource. It is not possible to reference by label, using the well-known *NameSelector or *NameRef patterns.

### How could Crossplane help solve your problem?
Add support for referenced fields in the database owner role.
